title: centaurea carratracensis
intro: Centaurea carratracensis, also known as Carratrac or Spanish Ironwort, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal effects due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea carratracensis are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
